entries.title: Framing Complete on Bellevue DADU
entries.alt: Double-stud DADU framing structure in Bellevue WA by NBE LLC
entries.caption: The main structural framing is complete on this 800 sq ft Bellevue backyard cottage. Next week we begin roof sheathing and rough-in plumbing.
entries.desc:
Our framing crew has successfully finalized the load-bearing double-stud wall assemblies for this custom 800-square-foot backyard DADU in Bellevue, WA.

We utilized premium Douglas fir framing material to ensure structural integrity and meet local seismic resistance requirements. The roof trusses have been engineered and hoisted into place, ready for sheathing and waterproof membrane application next week.

Subcontractors for rough-in plumbing and electrical have been scheduled for the following phase, moving this project ahead of its anticipated schedule.

entries.title: Subway Tile Install in Redmond Master Bath
entries.alt: Subway tile tilework in Redmond master bathroom remodel by NBE LLC
entries.caption: Precise subway tile installation in Redmond. We are using a custom dark grout pattern for a stunning modern look.
entries.desc:
Detailed craftsmanship on display in Redmond! Our lead tile setter is installing classic white subway tile in a dynamic vertical stack pattern.

To guarantee complete moisture protection, we applied a continuous Schluter-KERDI waterproofing membrane behind all tiled surfaces. We're finishing the look with a contrasting charcoal grout to match the client's choice of matte black fixtures.

Shower pan grading was validated with a water test prior to tile setting, ensuring optimal drainage and long-term durability.

entries.title: Cedar Siding Upgrades in Shoreline
entries.alt: Premium Western Red Cedar exterior siding installation in Shoreline WA by NBE LLC
entries.caption: Siding replacement project completed in Shoreline. Beautiful tongue-and-groove cedar adds incredible natural curb appeal.
entries.desc:
Exterior transformation in Shoreline, WA. We completed the removal of outdated composite vinyl siding and replaced it with premium clear vertical grain Western Red Cedar.

A high-performance weather-resistive barrier (HydroGap drainable housewrap) was installed beneath the cedar to provide an engineered rainscreen, preventing moisture trapping and wood rot.

The cedar was pre-stained on all six sides with a marine-grade UV-protective clear coat to preserve the rich, natural wood tones for years to come.

entries.title: Foundation Pour for Structural Addition in Kent
entries.alt: Concrete foundation pour for home addition in Kent WA by NBE LLC
entries.caption: Successful concrete foundation pour for a 500 sq ft structural home addition in Kent. The cure has begun.
entries.desc:
Today marked a major milestone for our Kent home addition project. We successfully poured and finished 22 cubic yards of 4,000 PSI high-strength concrete for the new structural foundation.

Our structural engineers verified the placement of heavy #5 steel rebar grids and anchor bolts prior to the truck arrivals. We also integrated a heavy-duty vapor barrier below the slab to block ground moisture transmission.

We are maintaining wet-cure protocols over the next 7 days to maximize structural compression strength before framing begins.

entries.title: Kitchen Demo Complete on Seattle Remodel
entries.alt: Kitchen demolition completed on historical Seattle home by NBE LLC
entries.caption: Demolition phase is wrapped up on this historical Seattle home. We are opening up the layout for a modern, open-concept kitchen.
entries.desc:
Demolition is complete for this Seattle kitchen remodel. All outdated cabinetry, plaster walls, and dual-layer flooring have been safely removed and recycled.

Due to the historical nature of the property, we constructed full dust containment zones using zip-wall barriers and deployed negative-air scrubbers to protect the rest of the home.

Next up: structural framing to insert a flush-mount steel LVL beam, eliminating the load-bearing wall to create a seamless transition to the dining room.

entries.title: Drywall and Taping Complete on Tacoma ADU
entries.alt: Drywall mudding and taping on interior walls in Tacoma ADU by NBE LLC
entries.caption: Tacoma ADU walls are now fully drywalled and taped to a Level 4 finish. Interior paint is scheduled for next week.
entries.desc:
Interior finishes are coming together on our Tacoma ADU project. The drywall installation is complete, and we have wrapped up taping and mudding to a clean, paint-ready Level 4 finish.

We utilized moisture-resistant 'greenboard' drywall in the bathroom and kitchen zones to offer advanced protection against humidity.

Our crews will sand down the joints and apply a high-quality primer coat before the custom trim and finish work begins.

entries.title: LVL Structural Beam Install in Mercer Island
entries.alt: Engineered LVL beam structural installation on Mercer Island home by NBE LLC
entries.caption: Removing a load-bearing column in Mercer Island and replacing it with a 24-foot engineered structural LVL beam.
entries.desc:
A complex engineering feat completed on Mercer Island. We successfully removed an obstructive load-bearing wall column and replaced it with a 24-foot multi-ply Laminated Veneer Lumber (LVL) beam.

Our crews erected temporary heavy-duty shoring walls to support the upper story load during the transition. The beam was seated on new triple-stud columns anchored into the foundation footings below.

Structural engineer inspects and signs off, giving the client a beautifully open, uninterrupted living and dining room layout.

entries.title: Custom Deck Decking Completed in Issaquah
entries.alt: Completed custom composite wood deck in Issaquah WA by NBE LLC
entries.caption: Completed composite deck installation in Issaquah. Maintenance-free decking with a stunning view.
entries.desc:
Stunning outdoor living expansion complete in Issaquah! We built a custom 450 sq ft multi-level deck utilizing top-tier Trex Transcend composite decking in a warm wood grain finish.

The deck frame is constructed with pressure-treated lumber and wrapped in deck-joist tape for maximum water diversion, extending the frame's lifetime. We installed a matte black powder-coated aluminum railing system for maximum safety without blocking the beautiful forest views.

Concealed fasteners were used for a flawless, screw-free surface that is safe for bare feet.
